stages.surface: Roughly 60% gravel: paved out of Roros (Femundsveien) and on Fv28 Kopparleden km 42-56; gravel on Gradalsveien seter road km 18-42 and the Oversjodalsfjellet mountain road km 56-74
stages.summary: Train Oslo S 06:28 -> Hamar 07:44, change to R60 Rorosbanen 08:11 -> Roros 11:38 (Entur, verified for Thu 9 Jul 2026). Shop at Coop Mega 100 m from the station, rolling by ~12:30. South-east out of Roros, up the Gradalen seter valley, down to Tufsingdalen (km 40), west on Fv28, then over the Oversjodalsfjellet gravel road to Oversjodalen. With July daylight past 23:00 a ~19:30 arrival is comfortable for fit riders.
highlights: Ellefsplass: converted house with electricity, running water and full bathroom
stages.notes: DRAFT FIX 1: 45-60 km is too short; it pushes day 2 to 105-120 km over the roughest ground on the route. Target km 75. DRAFT FIX 2: the route never goes near Narjordet (19 km off-route) or Os (11 km off-route); day 1 country is Gradalen/Tufsingdalen/Oversjodalen. Ellefsplass is a self-service DNT cabin exactly on the track at km 75: book on hyttebestilling.dnt.no, DNT membership + standard key required; provisions store in the cabin. Non-members: wild camp by Oversjodalen (easy, legal, 150 m from buildings). Resupply: Mauritz Landhandel / Naerbutikken Tufsingdal at km 40 (1.4 km off-route), staffed Mon-Sat 06:00-20:00 plus 24/7 self-service section, so it works even on a late pass-through. Water from streams all day. Bail-out if the train is late or Gradalen is slow: wild camp around Tufsingdalen km 40-45. Reserve bike spaces on both trains; R60 (SJ Norge) has limited slots.

stages.surface: Roughly 60% gravel and track: Orvdalen -> Storlagda is ~8 km of rough cart track/trail (hike-a-bike likely), then gravel through Nordre Solendalen; paved Fv665/Fv30 in Rendalen km 117-136; gravel Jutulhoggveien; final 16 km paved on Rv3
stages.summary: The wilderness crux of the whole route. Up Orvdalen on a cart track to the Storlagda seters and across the Rendalen Ostfjell plateau at ~1,000 m, long descent to the Rendalen valley floor (300 m), 13 km on Fv30, then the climb past the Jutulhogget canyon viewpoint (km 140) and Rv3 into Alvdal. Biggest day of the block: 88 km with ~2,000 m, mostly remote.
highlights: Jutulhogget, northern Europe's largest canyon, viewpoint directly on route at km 140
stages.notes: RESUPPLY WARNING: no shop on route between Tufsingdalen (km 40) and Alvdal (km 163); carry a full day's food from Roros/Tufsingdalen. The author's hike-a-bike warning bites here: UT.no describes the Orvdalen-Storlagda link as 'a reasonably good cart track/path, ca 8 km'; with loaded bikes budget walking pace for parts, slower when wet. Bail-out shop: Joker at Bergset (Rendalen) 3.1 km off-route from km 121, weekday hours (this is a Friday, open). Water from streams until Rendalen. Rv3 km 141-157 is the main Oslo-Trondheim truck route: shoulder riding, take care in Friday evening traffic. Alvdal (Fri evening): SPAR and Coop Extra, bike shop, doctor. Gjelten Bru Camping is 1.8 km off-route at km 166.5 (tent + cabins); wild camping before town also fine.

stages.surface: Mostly gravel: Solndalen seter road climbing out of Alvdal, short paved links past Folldal, then the Grimsdalsvegen gravel toll road (free for bikes) into Grimsdalen
stages.summary: Deliberate short day after the big one. One long climb out of Alvdal on the Solndalen seter road into the Dovre foothills, skirting Folldal, then up the Grimsdalsvegen toll road into Grimsdalen at 900-1,000 m between Rondane and Dovre national parks. Hut at km 226 (not km 230 as drafted), so 63 km, not 67.
highlights: Grimsdalen, one of Norway's finest seter valleys
Staffed DNT hut with served dinner and breakfast
stages.notes: Saturday: buy TWO days of food in Alvdal in the morning; Sunday closures follow and the only reliable Sunday shop ahead is Bunnpris Dombas. Folldal's shops (Coop Prix, Joker) are 6.5 km off-route each way at km 207: skip unless short. Grimsdalshytta 2026 summer season 19 Jun - 13 Sep (confirmed by DNT), book online ahead; tenting by the hut with bought meals is the fallback if beds are full.

stages.surface: Roughly 55% gravel: upper Grimsdalsvegen to the 1,108 m top at km 245, paved descent to Dovre and Dombas, valley link toward Lesja, then Sladalsvegen 32 km gravel toll road over the 1,130 m plateau, paved final descent to Vagamo
stages.summary: Continue up Grimsdalen to 1,108 m, fast 500 m descent to Dovre and Dombas (km 263), cross the Gudbrandsdalen/Lesja valley floor, then climb Sladalsvegen, the celebrated gravel road over the wild-reindeer plateau between Lesja and Vaga, topping out at ~1,130 m at km 290, and drop 750 m into Vagamo. 85 km (draft said 81; Dombas is km 263, Vaga km 311).
highlights: Sladalsvegen, 32 km of premier gravel, ride reports rate it among Norway's best
stages.notes: SUNDAY 12 JUL: groceries closed in Dombas and Vaga EXCEPT Bunnpris Dombas, open Sundays 11:00-20:00 (confirmed). Time Dombas arrival after 11:00 and buy lunch, dinner and Monday breakfast there. Vaga Coop Mega is shut Sundays; Vagamo has eateries and a fuel-station kiosk only. Sladalsvegen: no services and exposed above treeline for the 45 km Dombas -> Vaga; carry water and a wind layer; surface is good graded gravel, fully rideable loaded. Smedsmo Camping at km 312; hotels in Vagamo as backup.

stages.surface: Paved Fv51 climb past Lemonsjoen to Randsverk (km 335); gravel from Randsverk through Murudalen, then Hinoglslivegen and Jotunheimvegen (low-traffic gravel toll roads) along the Vinstre lakes
stages.summary: Hardest start of the trip: 600 m of climbing in the first 9 km out of Vagamo (370 m -> 982 m at km 320). Regroup at Randsverk (shop + cafe, km 335), then gravel through Murudalen, climbing gently to the Vinstre lakes at ~1,030 m with the Jotunheimen panorama ahead, joining the Mjolkevegen corridor. Ends at Haugseter Fjellstue, km 401. The southern half takes over from here (Bygdin km 414, Beitostolen km 427).
highlights: Climb past Lemonsjoen with Jotunheimen views
stages.notes: DRAFT FIX 3: Haugseter is at track km 401, not ~385; the stage is 90 km with ~1,750 m, not 75 km. Plan accordingly or wild camp earlier along Vinstre. Resupply: Coop Marked Randsverk km 335 is the ONLY shop today, open daily 09:00-18:00 in season (cafe next door); next grocery is Beitostolen km 427, so buy tomorrow's breakfast here. Storm-Hans damage on Hinoglslivegen at km 372: author's Feb 2025 note and caution POI say passable with one short hike-a-bike. Haugseter Fjellstue open 19 June through September, daily meals and lodging (confirmed on haugseter.no); book ahead, it fills in Besseggen season. Wild camping by Vinstre is the no-booking fallback.

food.name: Trygstad Bakeri og Konditori
food.description: The classic Roros bakery at Kjerkgata 12, baking since 1906 (not 1948 as often misquoted; official site: 'siden 1906'). Famous for skoleboller and solboller; ideal pre-departure carb load and pastries to pack for the first leg toward Gradalen. Open Mon-Fri 08:30-17:00, Sat 09:00-16:00, and (unusually for Norway) Sunday 11:30-16:30 thanks to Roros' tourist-town status.

food.name: Kaffestuggu
food.description: Roros' oldest eating house (Bergmannsgata 18), cafe-restaurant doing local produce with a modern twist: open sandwiches, sodd, reindeer dishes, good cakes and coffee. Open daily including Sundays (Mon-Wed and Sun 11:00-18:00, Thu-Sat 11:00-23:00). Reliable lunch or early dinner.

food.name: Vertshuset Roros
food.description: The best dinner in town: historic inn at Kjerkgata 34 serving regional Roros food (reindeer, local lamb, Rorosmeieriet dairy). Restaurant open Tue 17:00-23:00, Wed-Sat 12:00-16:00 and 17:00-23:00 (kitchen closes 21:30). Closed Sun-Mon, so fine for a Wed/Thu start night. Hours and operation confirmed on the official Roroshotellene page; booking engine live for summer 2026.

food.name: Skanckebua
food.description: Gastropub mid-way up Kjerkgata, run by Bergstadens Hotel, with an upstairs bar overlooking the street. Local meat and fish, burgers (the reindeer burger is a top seller), fish and chips, tacos; no pizza. Open Tue-Thu 12:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-02:00 (kitchen to 22:00); closed Sun-Mon, so time your pint for Tue-Sat. Confirmed operating via Bergstadens Hotel and Visit Roros.

food.name: Extra Roros (Coop)
food.description: Main supermarket for resupply, Coop Extra at Havsjoveien 3 on the southern edge of town, on the way out toward the route. Long hours: Mon-Sat 07:00-23:00 and Sun 10:00-23:00 (Roros holds a tourist-place Sunday-trading exemption; Sunday opening confirmed by two listing services). Stock up here: limited resupply until Alvdal at km 163.

sleep.name: Hittersjoen northeast tip (wild camp)
sleep.description: Shoreline at the far northeast tip of Hittersjoen lake, ~2.7 km NE of the town centre: follow Fv31 Brekkveien out of town along the north side of the lake, then lakeside tracks/paths (Borta Sjoa, Malmveien) to the NE end. Flat pine-heath pitches, swimmable lake. This spot is 300m+ from the nearest mapped building; note the east and south shores nearer town are lined with cabins (Sjohaga'an) and a farm (Strommevollen) sits NE of the lake, so do not pitch there. Legal under allemannsretten on uncultivated land, max 2 nights. No facilities; fill water in town. Do not pitch inside the UNESCO town itself.
sleep.bookingNote: No booking; allemannsretten rules apply (150m from inhabited houses, leave no trace).

sleep.name: Roros Camping Moen (ex Haneset Camping)
sleep.description: The town campsite at Havsjoveien 131, ~2 km southwest of the centre by the Glomma river. Tent pitches plus cabins and motel rooms: basic 4-person cabins from 660 NOK, small 2-person cabins 900-1100 NOK, larger serviced cabins with shower/WC 1190-1680 NOK, single motel room 560 NOK, motel family room 800 NOK (all confirmed on the official site). Reception 09-22, online booking on their site.
sleep.bookingNote: Tent pitches are normally walk-up fine; book cabins ahead for mid-July, Roros is a busy UNESCO destination in summer.

sleep.name: Rorosvidda Hotell (ex Idrettsparken)
sleep.description: The budget hotel option, formerly Idrettsparken Hotell (rebrand confirmed by local press and an active 2026 Booking.com listing), at Ora 25, a 4-minute walk from the historic centre and ~500m from the station. Simple rooms with private or shared bath, breakfast typically included, free parking and secure spot for bikes on request. Roughly 1000-1400 NOK per double depending on room type and date (aggregators show from ~$104-132).
sleep.bookingNote: Book 2-4 weeks ahead for mid-July; small property and Roros fills up in summer.

sleep.name: Vertshuset Roros (rooms)
sleep.description: Step-up option if you want one comfortable night before 705 km of gravel: historic inn rooms above the best restaurant in town, Kjerkgata 34, breakfast included (served Wed-Sun 07:30-10:00). Part of the Roroshotellene group; booking engine live for summer 2026.
sleep.bookingNote: Book ahead for mid-July; it is the most sought-after address in the historic centre.

towns.notes: Start-town logistics: you ride out Thursday 9 July, so Sunday closures do not bite in Roros itself, but note both Vertshuset's restaurant and Skanckebua are closed Sun-Mon if plans shift. Roros holds a tourist-place Sunday-trading exemption: Extra confirmed open Sundays 10:00-23:00 and Trygstad Bakeri Sundays 11:30-16:30. Do the full resupply at Extra on Havsjoveien on the way out: between Roros and Alvdal (km 163) via Tufsingdalen and Rendalen, shops are sparse and small. Trygstad opens 08:30 weekdays, early enough for a Thursday departure pastry run. If wild camping at Hittersjoen, use the NE tip of the lake (see entry); the shores nearer town are cabin-lined.
towns.verificationNotes: Adversarial check, June 2026, all 9 entries tested for existence, coordinates, mid-July opening, and price sanity. RESULT: 0 removed, 4 corrected, 5 confirmed as-is. (1) Trygstad Bakeri: exists at Kjerkgata 12, coords match OSM to the metre, hours incl. Sunday confirmed; FIXED founding year, official site says 'siden 1906', not 1948. (2) Kaffestuggu: fully confirmed (address, hours, coords exact vs OSM). (3) Vertshuset restaurant: hours and Sun-Mon closure confirmed verbatim on official Roroshotellene page; coords exact for Kjerkgata 34. (4) Skanckebua: exists, Bergstadens Hotel's gastropub, coords exact vs OSM POI; FIXED menu claim (no pizza; burgers confirmed, reindeer burger a top seller) and ADDED Sun-Mon closure (open Tue-Thu 12-23, Fri-Sat 12-02); price widened to 200-450 NOK per menu aggregator (range 200-600). (5) Extra Roros: exists at Havsjoveien 3, coords exact; hours Mon-Sat 07-23, Sun 10-23 confirmed on cited kassal.app listing. (6) Hittersjoen wild camp: FAILED coordinate check. Point 62.591,11.418 lies INSIDE the lake (point-in-polygon test against OSM water relation 4224637), and the described east-shore area has 8-14 cabins within 200m, violating the entry's own 150m claim; Sjohaga'an cabins line the shores nearer town and Strommevollen farm sits NE. REPLACED with computed land point 62.5904,11.4261 at the NE tip: verified on land, 300m+ from nearest mapped building (max-distance point on the shoreline), access via Fv31 Brekkveien plus lakeside paths. (7) Roros Camping: confirmed, official name 'Roros Camping Moen' (formerly Haneset Camping), address Havsjoveien 131 and ALL prices (380-500 tent, 560-1680 cabins/motel) matched the official site exactly; reception 09-22 confirmed; coords match OSM. (8) Rorosvidda Hotell: rebrand from Idrettsparken confirmed (local paper retten.no, active Booking.com 2026 listing); Ora 25 coords exact; price range consistent with aggregators (from ~$104-132). (9) Vertshuset rooms: operating, 2026 booking engine live; 1600-2200 NOK/double plausible for the property class, source switched to official English page. Sources: trygstadbakeri.no, kaffestuggu.no, roroshotellene.no, bergstadenshotel.no, kassal.app, roroscamping.no, booking.com, retten.no, visitnorway.com, Tripadvisor, OSM Nominatim/Overpass.

food.name: Ellefsplass provisions room (DNT matlager)
food.description: The hut itself is dinner: self-service DNT cabin with a stocked provisions room (tinned/dried meals, breakfast staples) plus full kitchen, electricity and running water. No staffed meals or meal times; you cook, then register and pay food in the DNT app. Open 1 Jun-10 Sep, so mid-July is fine. Non-guests may buy food without paying the day-visit fee (confirmed in the 2026 price list: payment only required if you also use the common room or kitchen).
food.price: Pantry items at DNT list prices, pay in app

food.name: Naerbutikken Tufsingdal (Mauritz Landhandel)
food.description: The resupply for this leg, on the route in Tufsingdal village roughly 20-25 km before Ellefsplass. Address Kopparleden 6075, 2555 Tufsingdalen, phone +47 477 00 083. Chain: Naerbutikken (NorgesGruppen), run by Mauritz Landhandel AS (org 920 776 213, active). Listed access 06:00-20:00 daily (Fri to 22:00) including Sundays via self-service/partially remote-operated shopping; staffed hours are shorter, and the self-checkout may want a Norwegian payment app, so aim for daytime. Also does parcel and medicine pickup. Last shop before the empty Orvdalen-Storlagda-Rendalen stretch.

food.name: Jonasvollen Camping kiosk (Femundsenden)
food.description: Lakeside kiosk at the south end of Lake Femunden, departure point for the MS Faemund II boat. Kiosk sales, fishing licences, boat hire and marina. Only worth it as a roughly 9-12 km detour east/southeast from Tufsingdal village for a snack stop and a look at Femunden; it is the only place resembling a cafe anywhere near this leg. The site itself is open year-round per jonasvollen.no, but kiosk hours vary, so call +47 414 03 216 or +47 62 49 89 94 before detouring.

sleep.name: DNT Ellefsplass (self-service cabin)
sleep.description: The stop itself: a converted farmhouse run by DNT Oslo og Omegn in Oversjodalen (Tolga municipality) with 8 beds, electricity, running water and a full bathroom on the ground floor, unusually plush for a self-service hut. Open 1 Jun-10 Sep annually (confirmed on ut.no). Bedding base provided, bring a sheet liner.
sleep.price: NOK 340 member / 520 non-member (2026 price list, confirmed)
sleep.bookingNote: Book ahead for mid-July: only 6 of 8 beds are pre-bookable on hyttebestilling.dnt.no (cabin id 10831), the other 2 are drop-in. Booked beds are released if you have not arrived by 19:00. Pay via DNT app. With 8 beds a booking is cheap insurance even if drop-in usually works on a Thursday.

sleep.name: Wild camp, south shore of Oversjoen tarn
sleep.description: Open heath and pine ground on the south/east shore of the Oversjoen tarn (Kartverket: tjern at 62.2279, 11.4854, Tolga), right beside the road you ride into Oversjodalen from Tufsingdalen, about 3 km northeast of Ellefsplass. Utmark, roughly 800 m from the nearest farm (Oversjoen gard at 62.2344, 11.4831), so comfortably legal under allemannsretten (150 m rule). Lake water for filtering, good swimming. Alternative: pitch beside the hut itself, but the 2026 DNT price list explicitly says campers using the cabin after 18:00 pay full lodging.
sleep.bookingNote: No booking. Standard allemannsretten rules: max 2 nights, leave no trace, no open fire 15 Apr-15 Sep.

sleep.name: Lillemoen holiday house, Oversjodalen
sleep.description: Fully renovated small farmhouse in the village, about 1 km east of Ellefsplass. Sleeps 5 (two double bedrooms plus a single loft bed), washing machine, dishwasher, full kitchen. Active Airbnb listing ('Lillemoen - Great Nature', Tolga), also promoted on visitnorway.com and roros.no. The comfortable fallback if the DNT hut is full or you want a proper indoor night.
sleep.price: Whole house, rate on Airbnb (not published; expect typical Norwegian whole-cabin pricing well above the DNT hut)
sleep.bookingNote: Book on Airbnb in advance; whole-house listings in this area go early for July.

sleep.name: Jonasvollen Camping
sleep.description: The nearest official campsite: caravan pitches, tent space and camping cabins at Femundsenden, the south tip of Lake Femunden, with marina, boat hire and kiosk. Open year-round per jonasvollen.no. Off-route, roughly 9-12 km east of Tufsingdal village (so about 25-30 km before Ellefsplass); only makes sense if you want a shorter day 1 and a lakeside night on Femunden rather than pushing to the hut.
sleep.price: Tent pitch price not published; small Norwegian sites typically NOK 150-300. Phone +47 414 03 216 / +47 62 49 89 94 (Kjell Magne) or +47 476 41 937 (Nina Alice)
sleep.bookingNote: No advance booking needed for a tent in mid-July; call ahead for a cabin.

towns.notes: This is the day 1 stop (Thu 9 July), ~65-75 km from Roros via Gradalen and Tufsingdalen, so the 12 July Sunday closure issue does not bite here; in any case Naerbutikken Tufsingdal has Sunday/evening self-service access. Key planning fact: Oversjodalen has no cafe, restaurant or shop. Buy dinner extras and day 2 food at Naerbutikken Tufsingdal (~20-25 km before the hut) or cook entirely from the Ellefsplass provisions room. Next on-route resupply after this is Rendalen/Alvdal (km 163), so leave Tufsingdalen loaded. Ellefsplass coordinates from Kartverket place-name register (62.20446, 11.44822). 2026 DNT Oslo og Omegn self-service prices confirmed from the official 2026 price PDF (adult NOK 340 member / 520 non-member). Bring DNT membership cards: pays for itself over the trip's hut nights.
towns.verificationNotes: All 7 entries survived adversarial checking; none removed. Verified: (1) Ellefsplass: ut.no/hytte/10831 confirms DNT Oslo og Omegn self-service cabin in Oversjodalen with provisions room, 8 beds (6 bookable), electricity, running water, full bathroom, open 1 Jun-10 Sep annually. Coordinates are an exact Kartverket match (turisthytte, Tolga: 62.20446, 11.44822). (2) 2026 prices: pulled the official PDF (dnt.no/globalassets/.../2026_priser_selvbetjent_noogen.pdf), text-extracted it: adult member 340, non-member 520, exactly as claimed. The PDF also confirms two secondary claims verbatim: food-only purchases at self-service huts require no day-visit payment, and campers using the hut after 18:00 pay full overnight. (3) Naerbutikken Tufsingdal: active (Mauritz Landhandel AS, org 920 776 213, on proff.no), kassal.app confirms address Kopparleden 6075 and hours Mon-Sun 06-20, Fri to 22; Nettavisen coverage confirms the round-the-clock self-service concept. Store coordinates sit ~1 km from the Kartverket Tufsingdalen farm point, consistent with a village stretched along Kopparleden: sane. Added phone 477 00 083. (4) Jonasvollen Camping: jonasvollen.no confirms kiosk, cabins, caravan pitches, marina, fishing licences, Faemund II departure point, and phone numbers (62 49 89 94 / 414 03 216 match the data). Coordinates match Kartverket (Jonasvollen, Engerdal: 62.23198, 11.87413). Corrected: open year-round per official site, not just summer-season; detour distance adjusted to ~9-12 km. (5) Wild camp: Kartverket confirms Oversjoen tjern in Tolga at 62.2279, 11.4854 and Oversjoen gard at 62.2344, 11.4831; camp point is on the SE shore, ~800 m from the farm (legal under the 150 m rule) and ~3 km from the hut (data said 2.5 km, fixed). (6) Lillemoen: Airbnb listing ID 816706160191663424 resolves to 'Lillemoen - Great Nature' in Tolga, sleeps 5, 2 double bedrooms + loft single, renovated, washer/dishwasher, corroborated by visitnorway.com and en.roros.no listings (40 km east of Tolga in Oversjodalen). Removed the unverifiable 'managed by Malmplassen' claim. Exact house coordinates could not be pinned to the metre but are within the village and consistent with '~1 km east of Ellefsplass'. Residual risks: Lillemoen July availability not checked (Airbnb calendar not fetched); Jonasvollen kiosk opening hours in any given week unverified (call ahead, as noted); Tufsingdal store coordinates accurate to village level (~1 km), not GPS-verified to the building.

food.name: Frich's Spiseri Alvdal
food.description: Roadside diner-restaurant on Rv3 at Nord-Østerdalsveien 5148, Steimosletta, next to Aukrustsenteret. Verified hours: cafe 08:00-23:00 daily, breakfast 07:00-10:00, dagens middag 12:00-20:00 (Sat to 19:00), a la carte to 21:30 (Sat 20:30). The reliable hot-meal option in Alvdal and open Sundays, unlike most of the town. Phone +47 62 48 80 00.

food.name: Huldra kafe (Huldra handverk, mat og kultur)
food.description: Craft shop and cafe at Steia 10 in Alvdal centre, confirmed operating: fresh yeast bakes, cakes, open sandwiches, simple hot dishes and popular soups. Daytime hours only (roughly 10:00-16:00, check their Facebook page) and closed Sundays, so it works for a Friday/Saturday arrival but not Sunday 12 July.

food.name: Solan og Ludvigs kafe at Aukrustsenteret
food.description: Cafe inside the Kjell Aukrust centre on Steimosletta, a few hundred metres from Frich's. Verified open daily 10:00-18:00 in July, including Sundays. Light meals, cake and coffee; combine with the Flaklypa exhibit if you have a rest hour.

food.name: Extra Alvdal (Coop Extra)
food.description: Main supermarket for resupply, Nord-Østerdalsveien 5152 on the Rv3/Steimosletta strip, 200 m north of Frich's. Coop's own page confirms the store (phone +47 62 48 72 00) but does not publish weekly hours; expect roughly 07:00-22:00 Mon-Sat and verify locally. Closed Sundays under Norwegian trading law, so closed 12 July. Stock up here before the final ~3 km out to Gjelten Bru Camping; next shop on the route is Folldal (~30 km), then nothing until Dombas.

food.name: Spar Alvdal
food.description: Second supermarket, at Steia 16 in the town centre a few doors from Huldra kafe. Confirmed operating (phone 62 48 76 00); also does hot food and take-away pizza. Closed Sundays. If you do hit Alvdal on Sunday 12 July, fallback food is Frich's (daily 08:00-23:00) or a fuel-station kiosk.

sleep.name: Gjelten Bru Camping
sleep.description: The stop itself: riverside campsite at Sølndalsveien 15, ~3.5 km northwest of Alvdal centre and directly on the route toward Folldal/Grimsdalen. Verified: 13 cabins, 45 powered pitches, modern sanitary block, kiosk, TV lounge, playground. Camping season 20 May - 1 Oct; cabins year-round. Coin showers (NOK 10+), washing machine NOK 60, bedding rental NOK 60. Cabin prices confirmed on the official site: NOK 490 (simple 4-bunk, bring sheets/liner), NOK 690 (insulated 2-room), NOK 990 (modern 6-berth with kitchen and own shower/WC).
sleep.price: Tent + 2 riders est. NOK 150-250 (not published, confirm on arrival); cabins NOK 490-990 verified
sleep.bookingNote: Site states no reservation needed for tents. For a cabin in mid-July, call/email a few days ahead: +47 973 23 588, post@gjeltenbrucamping.no.

sleep.name: Wild camp: riverbank pine flats near Gjelten bru
sleep.description: Flat pine-and-heath terraces along the riverbank ~400-500 m upstream of the Gjelten bridge, opposite side from the campsite. Caution: this point reverse-geocodes to the Masshaugen farm area (Plassen), so scout on the ground and keep the legal 150 m from any inhabited house or farm building; camp only on open forest utmark, max 2 nights under allemannsretten. River water available (treat it). National open-fire ban applies 15 April - 15 September; use a stove. Realistically only if the campsite is full or you want a free night, since the official site is a few hundred metres away with showers.
sleep.bookingNote: No booking; standard allemannsretten rules. Verify the exact pitch on arrival, terrain not desk-verifiable.

sleep.name: Steien Hotel og Skydsstation
sleep.description: Restored 1878 coaching inn at Steia 20 in Alvdal centre, the characterful splurge option: 12 unique rooms (21 beds), all with private shower/WC, breakfast included, 2-course dinner for guests from local ingredients, English-inspired garden. Confirmed operating with 2026 prices on Booking.com and Agoda. Phone 975 70 175, post@steienhotel.no.
sleep.price: Approx NOK 1,500-1,900 double incl. breakfast (verify at booking)
sleep.bookingNote: Book ahead for mid-July; only 12 rooms, weekends fill.

sleep.name: Frich's Hotell og Spiseri Alvdal
sleep.description: Motel-style rooms attached to the Frich's diner at Nord-Østerdalsveien 5148, Steimosletta. Functional rooms with private bath; food downstairs 08:00-23:00 and breakfast 07:00-10:00. The cheapest indoor bed in Alvdal and the practical bad-weather bailout. Confirmed listed on Booking.com, Trivago and aggregators.
sleep.bookingNote: Walk-in usually fine midweek, but book online for July weekends.

towns.notes: Timing: Alvdal is km 163, so on a Thursday 9 July start you most likely arrive Friday 10 or Saturday 11 July, before the Sunday closures. 12 July 2026 is indeed a Sunday: both supermarkets (Extra, Spar) closed; Frich's (daily 08:00-23:00) and Solan og Ludvigs kafe at Aukrustsenteret (10:00-18:00 daily in July) are the Sunday food options. Sequencing: town layout corrected from original data. Steia centre (Spar, Huldra, Steien Hotel) sits at ~62.108; the Rv3/Steimosletta strip (Frich's, Extra, Aukrustsenteret) is ~700 m further north at ~62.114-115; the campsite is ~3 km beyond that to the northwest. So Extra is the last shop you pass heading out, do the full resupply there; next shop is Folldal (~30 km, also closed Sunday), then nothing until Dombas (km 264). Campsite kiosk covers basics only. Grimsdalshytta (next night, staffed DNT) and Haugseter were out of scope for this stop and still need their own booking checks.
towns.verificationNotes: Adversarial check, 11 June 2026. All 9 entries survived existence and seasonality checks; none removed, no replacements needed. Verified against primary sources: gjeltenbrucamping.no (address, phone, email, season 20 May-1 Oct, 13 cabins/45 pitches, exact cabin prices 490/690/990, washing 60, shower coin 10+, no tent reservation needed), frich.no (hours incl. Saturday variations), aukrust.no (July 10:00-18:00 daily incl. Sundays), coop.no (Extra exists at Nord-Østerdalsveien 5152), spar.no (Spar exists at Steia 16), opplevalvdal.no (Huldra operating), Booking.com/Agoda search listings showing 2026 prices for Steien and Frich's hotel. Corrections applied: (1) Six coordinate fixes via OSM geocoding of verified street addresses; the original Steimosletta points (Frich's, Extra, Aukrustsenteret) were ~0.9-1.1 km too far south, centre points (Huldra, Spar, Steien) 240-370 m off. Campsite coordinates were correct. (2) Aukrustsenteret cafe is named Solan og Ludvigs kafe, not Solans Kafe. (3) Steien Hotel is at Steia 20, not 22; added 12 rooms/21 beds and direct contact details. (4) Frich's source URL frich.no/alvdal 404s on direct fetch; replaced with live frich.no/927. (5) Frich's hotel price raised to from ~NOK 1,100-1,400 (aggregator floor ~US$134). (6) Wild camp point reverse-geocodes to Masshaugen farm area, not 'farm buildings at Gjelten' as claimed; description rewritten to require on-arrival scouting and 150 m setback. Residual unverified items: campsite tent price (not published, estimate flagged), Extra's exact weekly hours (blank on Coop's page), Huldra's precise hours (check Facebook), Steien rate range (plausible but confirm at booking), wild camp terrain (not desk-verifiable). Original claim of campsite river swimming/fishing not confirmed on the official page and dropped.

food.name: Grimsdalshytta dining room (DNT staffed hut)
food.description: The hut itself is the food stop: staffed summer season confirmed 19 Jun-13 Sep 2026 on the DNT English page, so open Sunday 12 July. Three-course dinner served at 19:00 every day (tell the warden on arrival; non-residents and tenters can eat if there is space). Day service 12:00-16:00 confirmed: soup, blings, roemmevafler (sour-cream waffles) and cakes, plus drinks and kiosk items; the hut also does stone-oven-baked pizza. All food made from scratch with local ingredients. Breakfast 190 NOK member / 253 non-member. Note DNT rule (verbatim in the 2026 price sheet): no eating your own food inside the hut, and own stoves cannot be used at the hut even outdoors.
food.price: Dinner 470 NOK member / 608 non-member; plate dish 258/300; waffles+coffee ~100

food.name: Grimsbu Turistsenter kafeteria
food.description: Roadside cafeteria at the eastern toll gate of Grimsdalsvegen (route km ~205, the last services before the 25 km climb to the hut). Open year-round; posted hours Mon-Fri 08:00-22:00, Sat-Sun 09:00-19:00 (so open Sunday 12 July, confirm on the day). On-site supermarket/kiosk and guest kitchen too. Honest truck-stop style rather than destination food, but reliable hot meals and the only kitchen between Alvdal and the hut. Phone +47 62 49 35 29.

food.name: Coop Prix Folldal
food.description: Backup grocery in Folldal centre (Dovreveien 2), but it is an 8-9 km off-route detour west of Grimsbu along Rv29 (16-18 km round trip). Mon-Fri 07:00-23:00, Sat 08:00-21:00, closed Sundays, so useless on 12 July. Only worth it if you missed Alvdal and need proper resupply before the valley.

food.name: Toftemo Turiststasjon cafe
food.description: Next-morning option on the descent to Dombaas: classic tourist station on the E6 at Vestsidevegen 1, Dovre, about 2 km north of Dovre village and ~9.5 km south of Dombaas (route km ~255). Cafeteria seats 75, a la carte menu plus breakfast/lunch service, homemade food. Good first coffee after rolling out of Grimsdalen. Phone +47 61 24 00 45 to confirm morning hours.
food.price: Mains ~180-280 NOK, coffee and waffles less

sleep.name: Grimsdalshytta (DNT Oslo og Omegn staffed hut)
sleep.description: 54 beds at 1,000 m between Rondane and Dovre national parks, right on the route at km 230. Staffed summer season 19 June - 13 September 2026 confirmed on the DNT site, so fully operating mid-July. Shower, drying room, food service, card payment. Bed-only rates (incl. shower) per the official 2026 DNT Oslo og Omegn price sheet: dorm 290 NOK member / 382 non-member, 4-6 bed room 445/578, 1-3 bed room 515/670. Full pension (bed, breakfast, 3-course dinner, packed lunch of 4 bread slices) 1,095/1,430 in dorm, up to 1,315/1,710 in 1-3 bed room. Sheet liner required, rent 114 NOK member / 130 non-member; own sleeping bags not allowed as bedding.
sleep.price: Dorm 290-382 NOK bed only; full pension 1,095-1,430 NOK pp (dorm)
sleep.bookingNote: Book online at hyttebestilling.dnt.no; mid-July is peak and reserving is sensible, but DNT staffed huts never turn anyone away (floor mattress 186/300 NOK, only offered when all beds taken). The self-service annex (19 beds) closes permanently after Easter 2026 as a wild-reindeer measure; the staffed summer operation continues unaffected. Contact: grimsdalshytta@dnt.no, +47 45 29 81 55.

sleep.name: Grimsdalshytta teltplass (tent pitch at the hut)
sleep.description: The hut has a designated tent area (teltplass listed among its 14 facilities on ut.no). 125 NOK member / 160 non-member per person (official 2026 price sheet line: teltere/tilgang til hyttefasiliteter) buys access to hut facilities including shower and toilets. Best-value play for two riders with a tent: pitch here, then buy the 3-course dinner and breakfast inside. Remember you cannot eat brought food inside the hut or run your own stove at the hut itself.
sleep.bookingNote: No booking needed for tenting; pay the warden. Book dinner on arrival (served 19:00).

sleep.name: Wild camp: Grimsa riverbank flats east of the hut
sleep.description: Open heath and grass flats along the Grimsa river, roughly 1.5-2 km east (back) along Grimsdalsvegen from the hut, well over 150 m from the hut and the nearest seter buildings. The Grimsdalen landskapsvernomraade regulation (verneplan for Rondane, vedlegg 3, 122.5 km2) restricts motor traffic and caravan parking but does not prohibit ordinary tenting, so normal allemannsretten applies. Flat pitches, river water, fishing in the Grimsa (licence needed). Free; no facilities. Cook here rather than at the hut.
sleep.bookingNote: No booking; standard allemannsretten rules (150 m from inhabited buildings, leave no trace). Move on after a night or two and avoid disturbing grazing animals and reindeer.

sleep.name: Grimsbu Turistsenter Motell & Camp
sleep.description: Fallback if you stop short of the hut: full-service campsite at the eastern toll-road entrance, route km ~205 (25-30 km and most of the climb before Grimsdalshytta). Cabins in all sizes (large ones with shower/WC), motel rooms, tent and caravan pitches, cafeteria, on-site supermarket, communal kitchen, sanitary block, bathing lake, playground. Open year-round; listed on camping.no and ut.no.
sleep.price: Tent ~300 NOK for 2; basic cabins from ~600 NOK, en-suite cabins more (confirm on grimsbu.no)
sleep.bookingNote: Drop-in fine for tents; phone +47 62 49 35 29 or mail@grimsbu.no for cabins in July.

towns.notes: Remote stop: plan around the hut. Grimsdalshytta is confirmed staffed 19 June - 13 September 2026, so it covers Sunday 12 July when groceries elsewhere shut (Coop Prix Folldal closed Sundays; Extra Alvdal at km 163 is listed Sunday-open 10:00-21:00 self-checkout but verify; Grimsbu cafeteria/store open Sundays 09:00-19:00). Last full supermarket on route is Extra Alvdal, km 163; after that only Grimsbu Turistsenter at km ~205. Grimsdalsvegen is a private toll road (bikes free), normally open from early June. Key DNT quirks for bikepackers, verbatim from the 2026 price sheet: no brought food eaten inside staffed huts and own stoves cannot be used at the huts even outdoors, so either pay for meals or cook at a wild camp away from it. Day service at the hut runs 12:00-16:00 (soup, waffles, cakes, stone-oven pizza); dinner is a single 3-course sitting at 19:00. The self-service annex closes permanently after Easter 2026 (wild reindeer measure) but this does not affect the staffed summer season. 2026 prices are official DNT Oslo og Omegn rates (https://www.dnt.no/globalassets/foreninger/dnt-oslo-og-omegn/priser/2026_priser_betjente-hytter_gjester_no.pdf). Hut contact: grimsdalshytta@dnt.no, +47 45 29 81 55.
towns.verificationNotes: Adversarial check completed 11 Jun 2026; all 9 entries survive, none removed, no replacements needed. Methods and findings: (1) Grimsdalshytta: DNT English page confirms summer season 19 Jun-13 Sep 2026 and 54 beds; the official 2026 DNT Oslo og Omegn price PDF was downloaded and read, confirming every quoted figure exactly (dorm 290/382, 4-6 bed 445/578, 1-3 bed 515/670, floor mattress 186/300, tent access 125/160, breakfast 190/253, dinner 470/608, plate dish 258/300, pension 1,095-1,315 member / 1,430-1,710 non-member) and the no-own-food/no-own-stove rule verbatim. Sheet liner corrected to 114 member / 130 non-member. Coordinates cross-checked against Peakbook UTM 32V 533498 6884452, which converts to 62.0907, 9.6415: within 60 m of the data, kept. DNT serving page confirms day service 12:00-16:00 (soup, blings, roemmevafler, cakes) and 19:00 three-course dinner; pizza corrected from wood-fired to stone-oven-baked. Annex permanent closure after Easter 2026 confirmed via ut.no. (2) Grimsbu Turistsenter: site live, phone +47 62 49 35 29 matches, cafeteria plus on-site supermarket confirmed, year-round operation confirmed via camping.no/ut.no listings, hours Mon-Fri 08-22 and weekends 09-19 per their Facebook; coordinates match Kartverket's Grimsbu village point (62.15605, 10.17309) within 150 m. Cabin counts (24/8) could not be confirmed, so softened to 'cabins in all sizes'. (3) Extra Alvdal: store confirmed on coop.no (Nord-Oesterdalsveien 5152); coordinates were ~1.1 km off and corrected to the Kartverket address point 62.1152, 10.6160; Sunday 10-21 opening supported by a third-party listing (Ernaer), kept with the existing verify caveat since coop.no's own hours table failed to render. (4) Coop Prix Folldal: store confirmed; address is Dovreveien 2 in Folldal centre (coop.no's page also shows Gruvvegen 2; both central); hours independently confirmed as Mon-Fri 07-23, Sat 08-21, closed Sundays, matching the data; coordinates within ~250 m of Kartverket points for central Folldal, kept. (5) Toftemo Turiststasjon: confirmed live at Vestsidevegen 1, 2662 Dovre; Kartverket geocodes that address to 61.99950, 9.22104, matching the data; cafeteria (75 seats), a la carte and breakfast/lunch confirmed; exact daily hours not published, so a phone-ahead note was added. (6) Wild camp: the cited Lovdata regulation exists (verneplan for Rondane vedlegg 3, Grimsdalen landskapsvernomraade, 122.5 km2) and contains no prohibition on ordinary tenting; the 'organised camps need permission' phrasing was not explicitly verifiable in the fetched text, so the entry now states only that tenting is not prohibited and allemannsretten applies. Coordinates ~1.9 km ESE of the hut along the valley floor: sane. Residual risks: Extra Alvdal Sunday hours and Grimsbu weekend cafeteria hours rest on third-party/Facebook listings, both flagged in the entries; Grimsbu cabin pricing is an estimate pending confirmation.

food.name: Bokhandel'n Bakeri & Mathus (Gjestehus)
food.description: The standout food stop in Vagamo: stone-oven bakery, pizza, burgers and pulled-pork sandwiches in a historic timber bookshop at Vagavegen 45 on the village square, run with Classic Norway Hotels as Bokhandel'n Gjestehus. Google 4.2 (396 reviews), verified. Current listed hours verified: Fri-Sat 10:00-22:00, Sun 12:00-20:00, Mon-Tue 10:00-17:00, Wed-Thu closed (likely extends to daily in peak July, but do not count on Wed-Thu). Confirmed OPEN Sunday, so this is your Sunday 12 July dinner plan. 700 m from Smedsmo Camping.

food.name: Cafe Edvard at Ullinsvin
food.description: Summer cafe in the old vicarage (Edvard Munch family connection) above Vaga church, with gallery, historic garden and local-food shop, good for coffee, home baking and snacks. 2026 season verified on ullinsvin.no: Cafe Edvard opens 20 June 2026, summer exhibitions run 21 June to 7 September and are open daily, so it works for a Sunday 12 July lunch. Typical hours 11:00-17:00 (exact 2026 daily hours not yet published, confirm on arrival). 10 min walk uphill from the centre.

food.name: Villa Vaga restaurant
food.description: Restaurant of the village hotel (Classic Norway Hotels), in the centre. Official site confirms a la carte menu in the summer season (the buffet/set-dish service is winter, for hotel guests). The reliable sit-down dinner if Bokhandel'n is full; non-guest dining not explicitly stated, call ahead (+47 99 49 25 35). Operating 2026.

food.name: Isbaren i Vaga
food.description: Ice-cream and small-dishes bar at Moavegen 1 in the village centre, dine-in and outdoor seating, active Facebook presence. Seasonal; July daily hours unverified, check Facebook. Backup if closed Sunday: Visit Vaga isbar/Vagais at Vagavegen 51A, a combined tourist office, ice-cream bar (local Vagais factory) and bakery outlet.

food.name: KIWI Vaga
food.description: Main resupply, KIWI chain, Skogbygdsvegen 2 just off the Rv15 roundabout, 600 m from Smedsmo. Hours verified: Mon-Fri 07:00-23:00, Sat 09:00-21:00, CLOSED Sunday. If you arrive Sunday 12 July, buy Saturday or wait until 07:00 Monday before the Murudalen climb. Second supermarket: Coop Mega Vaga, Vagavegen 47 (Mon-Fri 07:00-23:00, Sat 08:00-21:00, also closed Sunday). Sunday fallback for basics: Circle K Vaga fuel station, Vagavegen 36 on Rv15. Last full supermarket before Beitostolen on this route, so load up for the Vinstre/Bygdin leg.

sleep.name: Smedsmo Camping
sleep.description: The stop itself: family-run campsite beside Rv15 on the edge of Vagamo (Vagavegen 80), 700 m from the square. Coordinates verified against NorCamp (61.869778, 9.103056). Tent pitches on flat ground, service building with showers, kitchen, washing machines, WiFi. Prices verified on smedsmo.no: tent 230 NOK (280 with electricity), tent + car 320 NOK; 1-room cabin (3-4 pers) 690 NOK + 290 washout, 2-room (4 pers) 790 NOK + 340 washout, flats 1150 NOK + 420 washout. Open through summer (service building closes only Nov-Easter). Tent pitches first-come; cabins for mid-July 2026 bookable now and worth reserving, Vagamo is busy Jotunheimen-gateway territory in fellesferie.
sleep.price: Tent 230-320 NOK (2 riders + tent), cabin 690-790 NOK + washout 290-340
sleep.bookingNote: Tents: no booking needed (first-come). Cabins: book ahead for mid-July at reservations.visbook.com/6978.

sleep.name: Wild camp: Vagavatnet south shore west of Klones
sleep.description: Plausible allemannsretten spot: forested shoreline on the quiet south side of Vagavatnet, west of the Klones farm peninsula (Klones confirmed on the lake shore near the village, reached over Finnbrue or via the side road off Rv15 about 2 km out). Pine/birch woods and lake access; stay 150+ m from buildings and off cultivated fields around Klones. Uncultivated outfield, one to two nights fine; no facilities, take water from the lake or fill bottles in town. Coordinates approximate and geometrically consistent with the south shore; pick your exact pitch on the ground.
sleep.bookingNote: No booking; standard allemannsretten rules (150 m from dwellings, max 2 nights, leave no trace).

sleep.name: Villa Vaga (Classic Norway Hotels)
sleep.description: The mid-range hotel option: historic village hotel in the centre of Vagamo, 67 rooms, restaurant, bar, indoor pool, sauna, breakfast included. Verified operating 2026 (villavaga.no, Booking.com). Aggregator rates from USD 157-183, so budget roughly 1500-2000+ NOK per night for a July double; the original 1300-1800 estimate was optimistic for fellesferie.
sleep.bookingNote: Book ahead for mid-July; it is the main hotel in a Jotunheimen gateway town and fills in fellesferie.

sleep.name: Bokhandel'n Gjestehus
sleep.description: Guesthouse rooms above the bakery/restaurant at Vagavegen 45 on the square: listed on Booking.com as Bokhandel'n - by Classic Norway Hotels (4-star), family rooms with private bath, garden, restaurant downstairs, 24h front desk. Reclassified from hostel to hotel: this is a small upscale guesthouse, not budget dorm accommodation. Rates about USD 167-250, roughly 1700-2600 NOK. Pricier than Villa Vaga but you sleep above the best food in town.
sleep.bookingNote: Book ahead for mid-July 2026 via Booking.com; only a handful of rooms.

towns.notes: Timing: Vaga is km 311, which for two fit riders starting Roros Thu 9 July lands here Sat 11 or Sun 12 July. The Sunday risk is groceries only: KIWI and Coop Mega are closed Sun 12 July (verified), but Bokhandel'n is confirmed open Sunday 12:00-20:00, Cafe Edvard at Ullinsvin runs daily through the summer season (2026 season opens 20 June), and the Villa Vaga restaurant serves a la carte in summer. Circle K Vaga (Vagavegen 36, Rv15) covers emergency basics on Sunday. Resupply logic: KIWI Vaga is the last full supermarket before Beitostolen (km 427); the Vinstre/Bygdin leg over Murudalen has no shops, so carry a day-plus of food out of Vaga. If you arrive Sunday, either shop Saturday or wait for KIWI's 07:00 Monday opening before the climb. Smedsmo Camping coordinates are confirmed (NorCamp 61.869778, 9.103056, Vagavegen 80); the earlier camping.info discrepancy is resolved in favour of the NorCamp position. Summer 2026 cabin booking is open on Visbook; tent pitches are first-come and rarely full for two cyclists. Wild camp coordinates remain approximate, verify on the ground.
towns.verificationNotes: Adversarial pass on 2026-06-11 via live web search and direct fetches of smedsmo.no, norcamp.de, ullinsvin.no, villavaga.no, restaurantguru.com and Norwegian directory listings. All 5 food and 4 sleep entries survive; none removed, no replacements needed. VERIFIED EXACTLY: Smedsmo prices (tent 230/280/320, cabins 690+290 and 790+340, flat 1150), coordinates (NorCamp 61.869778/9.103056, Vagavegen 80) and Visbook 6978 booking link; Bokhandel'n hours including Sunday 12:00-20:00 and Google 4.2/396 reviews; KIWI hours (Mon-Fri 07-23, Sat 09-21, Sun closed); Coop Mega Vaga at Vagavegen 47; Circle K at Vagavegen 36; Villa Vaga and Bokhandel'n Gjestehus both live on Booking.com under Classic Norway Hotels. CORRECTED: (1) Cafe Edvard 2026 opening is 20 June, not 21 June; daily 11-17 hours not yet published for 2026, flagged as typical. (2) Villa Vaga restaurant serves a la carte in summer per its own site; the claimed hot/cold buffet is the off-season guest arrangement; non-guest access softened to call-ahead. (3) Villa Vaga double price raised to ~1500-2000 NOK (aggregators show from USD 157-183, original 1300-1800 was low for July). (4) Bokhandel'n Gjestehus retyped hostel to hotel (4-star guesthouse, private baths). (5) Smedsmo 'open year-round' qualified: service building closes Nov-Easter, irrelevant for July. (6) Smedsmo coordinate ambiguity in original notes resolved, camping.info position discarded. STILL UNVERIFIED (flagged in entries): Isbaren i Vaga July daily hours (exists, active Facebook, Moavegen 1); Bokhandel'n peak-July Wed-Thu opening; wild camp pitch quality (coordinates geometrically consistent with Vagavatnet south shore west of Klones, which is confirmed on the lake); 'last supermarket before Beitostolen' route claim taken on trust from route data.

food.name: Haugseter Fjellstue restaurant
food.description: The lodge itself is the only food at this stop and it is genuinely good: kitchen built on game, trout from Vinstervatn (the house speciality), local cured meats and stolslapper (mountain pancakes) with jam. Open every day in season (19 June to 27 Sept 2026, confirmed on haugseter.no), restaurant 11:00-20:00, lunch from 11:00, dinner menu 15:00-20:00; call ahead for table reservations. Drop-in travellers welcome (it is the classic road stop on Jotunheimvegen / Mjolkevegen cycle route). Open Sundays, so 12 July is no problem. Breakfast 220 NOK, packed lunch 85 NOK. Generator off 23:00-07:00.
food.price: Breakfast 220 NOK, packed lunch 85 NOK (both confirmed); dinner mains not published, budget ~250-400 NOK

food.name: Bygdin sommerkafe (Bygdin Hoifieldshotel)
food.description: Next food on the route, about 11 km past Haugseter where Jotunheimvegen meets Rv51 at the east end of lake Bygdin (NOT 29 km: Haugseter's own site puts it 24 km from Beitostolen, and Bygdin sits 13 km from Beitostolen between them). Summer cafe open 12 June through the first week of October, daily 11:00-18:00: waffles, rommegrot, daily soup, hot and cold dishes and daily dinner plates (children's menu too). Hotel restaurant serves 3-course dinners in the evening (book). Good morning-coffee target if you sleep at Haugseter.
food.price: Cafe prices not published; ~100-150 NOK waffles/soup, ~200-300 NOK hot dishes is a reasonable budget

food.name: KIWI 243 Beitostolen
food.description: Main resupply in Beitostolen (Ovrevegen 4A; 13 km down Rv51 from Bygdin, fast descent; ~24 km from Haugseter). Full-size supermarket and, critically, Sunday-open under the tourist-resort exemption: Sundays 09:00-21:00, Mon-Sat 08:00-23:00 (confirmed on kiwi.no, not 07:00 as previously listed). So even if you hit Beitostolen on Sunday 12 July you can resupply. There is no shop of any kind at Haugseter or Bygdin; last grocery before this stop is in Vaga, so carry food for the Murudalen-Vinstre leg.

sleep.name: Wild camp, Vinstre north shore east of Haugseter
sleep.description: Open mountain utmark at ~1,030 m along Jotunheimvegen, about 1-1.5 km east of Haugseter on the north shore of Vinstre: flat heath and grass down to the lake, easy water access, big views. Fully legal under allemannsretten; OSM reverse-geocodes the spot to the Perslettstolen seter area (isolated dwellings only), so pitching 150 m+ from cabins is easy. Exposed above treeline, so stake well. Fishing permits and an evening meal available 15-20 min walk away at Haugseter.

sleep.name: Haugseter Fjellstue tent pitch
sleep.description: The fjellstue takes tents on its grounds: 415 NOK per tent per night, shower 69 NOK (both confirmed on haugseter.no; motorhome 525 NOK), restaurant and limited facilities on site. The pragmatic choice: camp cheap, eat the game-and-trout dinner inside. Generator off 23:00-07:00, so charge electronics at dinner.
sleep.bookingNote: Tent pitches are not bookable online; turning up before the restaurant closes at 20:00 is the practical approach, or call +47 61 34 15 10 to be safe.

sleep.name: Haugseter Fjellstue (rooms and cabins)
sleep.description: Family-run staffed mountain lodge on the north shore of Vinstervatn, 60 beds across rooms, apartments and cabins (ut.no confirms 60 beds, betjent). Private, not DNT-run, but ut.no states it gives a discount to DNT members, so bring your card. Room with breakfast 1,429 NOK pp; half board (breakfast, packed lunch, dinner) 1,839 NOK pp; cabins 1,390-2,235 NOK per night plus 319 NOK pp bedding/towels (all confirmed on haugseter.no). Season 19 June to 27 September 2026 confirmed on their own site, so mid-July is squarely in season.
sleep.price: B&B 1,429 NOK pp; half board 1,839 NOK pp; cabins 1,390-2,235 NOK/night + 319 NOK bedding
sleep.bookingNote: Book ahead for a bed in mid-July: only 60 beds and it fills with Mjolkevegen cyclists, anglers and Jotunheimen hikers. Phone +47 61 34 15 10 or haugseter.fjellstua@gmail.com (both confirmed).

sleep.name: Bygdin Hoifieldshotel
sleep.description: Historic mountain hotel at the east end of lake Bygdin, oldest wing built 1897 (confirmed on bygdin.com history page), ~11 km past Haugseter: the fallback if Haugseter is full or you want to bank a few more km. Summer season early June through the first week of October (their own info page), so mid-July is open. Proper restaurant with 3-course evening dinners, plus the daytime sommerkafe 11:00-18:00. Half-board rates require 2+ night stays (confirmed); single nights room-and-breakfast, bookable direct or via booking.com (2026 rates live, doubles from roughly 1,700 NOK).
sleep.price: Roughly 1,500-2,500 NOK/double B&B depending on room; half board only for 2+ nights
sleep.bookingNote: Book ahead for mid-July weekends: +47 61 34 14 00 or booking@bygdin.com (both confirmed on bygdin.com). Listed with 2026 prices on booking.com.

towns.notes: Remote stop: Haugseter Fjellstue sits at ~1,030 m on the Jotunheimvegen toll gravel road, north shore of Vinstervatn, 24 km from Beitostolen and 12 km from Valdresflye (distances from haugseter.no itself). Season 19 June-27 September 2026 is confirmed on haugseter.no, so a 12-14 July arrival is fine, and it serves food every day including Sundays. Route correction: Bygdin is only ~11 km west of Haugseter (not 29 km), then 13 km down Rv51 to Beitostolen; downstream cumulative km marks should shift ~18 km earlier (Bygdin ~km 396, Beitostolen ~km 409 if Haugseter is km 385). There is zero retail between Vaga and Beitostolen: carry lunch food for the Murudalen-Vinstre leg and rely on the fjellstue for dinner (kitchen open to 20:00, plan arrival accordingly). The Sunday-closure problem does not bite here: Haugseter serves daily and KIWI Beitostolen is Sunday-open 09:00-21:00 under the resort exemption. No DNT hut is on-route (nearest staffed DNT, Fondsbu, is at the far west end of lake Bygdin, well off the track), but note Haugseter itself gives DNT members a discount per ut.no, so membership is worth something at this stop after all. Generator at Haugseter is off 23:00-07:00: charge devices during dinner. Coordinates verified against OpenStreetMap, accurate to ~100 m.
towns.verificationNotes: All 3 food and 4 sleep entries survive verification; none removed, so no replacements needed. Corrections made: (1) Distance error: Bygdin is ~11 km from Haugseter, not 29 km. Haugseter's own site (haugseter.no/english) states it is 24 km from Beitostolen; Bygdin lies between them, 13 km from Beitostolen on Rv51. Route km marks adjusted in notes. (2) DNT claim reversed: ut.no/hytte/10817 explicitly says Haugseter 'gir rabatt til DNT-medlemmer'; original data claimed no member discount. (3) KIWI weekday hours: 08:00-23:00 Mon-Sat per kiwi.no, not 07:00-23:00; Sunday 09:00-21:00 confirmed. (4) Bygdin sommerkafe season: 12 June through first week of October per bygdin.com, slightly longer than claimed; 'Bygdin burger' and 'hot food until 17:30' not found on their site, removed; waffles, rommegrot, soup, daily dinners confirmed. (5) Bygdin/cafe coordinates nudged ~120 m to OSM hamlet position (61.3276, 8.8011). (6) Wild camp nearest seter is Perslettstolen per OSM reverse geocode (was 'Liastolen'); point confirmed on land in Oystre Slidre utmark. Confirmed verbatim on primary sources: Haugseter season 19 Jun-27 Sep 2026, restaurant 11:00-20:00, dinner from 15:00, generator off 23:00-07:00, breakfast 220, packed lunch 85, tent 415, shower 69, B&B 1,429 pp, half board 1,839 pp, cabins 1,390-2,235 + 319 bedding, phone/email; kitchen game/Vinstervatn trout/stolslapper; 60 beds and betjent status (ut.no); Haugseter coordinates exact OSM match (61.34237, 8.98395); Bygdin hotel name 'Bygdin Hoifieldshotel' is their own official spelling, built 1897 (bygdin.com history page), summer season early June-first week Oct, phone +47 61 34 14 00 and booking@bygdin.com, half board 2+ nights only, 2026-bookable on booking.com (doubles from ~$166); KIWI 243 at Ovrevegen 4A geocodes to the listed coordinates. Unverified but plausible, kept with hedged wording: Haugseter dinner-main prices, Bygdin cafe prices, tent walk-up policy.

pois.name: Røros Bergstaden & Røros kirke (Bergstadens Ziir)
pois.description: UNESCO World Heritage mining town (listed 1980), founded on copper in 1644; the start line. The 1784 baroque church 'Bergstadens Ziir' seats about 1,600 and is one of Norway's largest churches. Entry NOK 60 (under 16 free with an adult); summer opening Tue-Sat 11:00-16:00. Organ concerts at 18:00 most evenings from 23 June (about 45 min, NOK 100 at the door, under 12 free). Entry is via the side doors 8 June-11 July due to main-door renovation, so this applies on the 9 July start day. Wandering Kjerkgata and Sleggveien's wooden houses takes 30-45 min and costs nothing.
pois.detour: On route, km 0 (route starts at Røros station, about 200 m away)

pois.name: Smelthytta, Røros Museum
pois.description: The rebuilt copper smelter at Malmplassen, now the main museum: working scale models of the mines, smelting process and water-powered hoists. Published schedule (2025; 2026 not yet posted): open daily 10:00-17:00 from 20 June to 15 August; adult NOK 150, under 16 free with an adult, audioguide (NO/EN/DE/FR) included. Allow about 1 hour. With the church and slag heaps this is the core of a 2-3 hour Røros morning before rolling out.
pois.detour: On route, km 0.1 (about 200 m off track)

pois.name: Slegghaugan slag heaps & Malmplassen
pois.description: Black hills of smelting slag from 333 years of copper production (1644-1977), protected as part of the World Heritage site. Climb to the top for the best free panorama over the turf-roofed town and church; 15-20 min, always open, no ticket.
pois.detour: 300 m off route at km 0.1

pois.name: Olavsgruva mine tour (Storwartz)
pois.description: Guided underground tour 50 m down and 500 m in to the Miners' Hall, through the old Nyberget mine (1650s) and the newer Olav's Mine. About 1 hour, in Norwegian and English; adult NOK 220, student/senior NOK 200 (2025 prices), under 16 free with an adult. The published 2026 schedule shows 15:00 daily for 1-19 June, with July times not yet posted at time of research, so book ahead online or on +47 72 40 61 70 (phone 10:00-15:00 daily). Mine stays cold year-round, bring a layer. Verdict: the one Røros sight that does not fit a 2-3 hour town visit; it costs a half day since it sits 13 km north-east of town, away from the route.
pois.detour: 13 km each way by road from Røros, opposite direction to the route; realistically a half-day add-on

pois.name: Grådalen seter valley
pois.description: The route's opening act builds in two stages: tarmac then gravel up Hådalen out of Røros, and from km 18 the track turns onto Grådalsveien, a toll gravel road (cars pay, bikes free) signed towards Grådalen and Grådalssetra. From the south end of Rambergsjøen it crosses open bare-mountain seter country, a local gravel classic, before dropping into Tufsingdalen around km 40. Active grazing, big skies, no services; the farms here do not run regular cafes, so this is a ride-through landscape rather than a stop. Verified on track roughly km 18-40, topping out around 740-790 m.
pois.detour: On route, km 18-40

pois.name: Tufsingdalen & Mauritz Landhandel
pois.description: Quiet farming valley in Os municipality west of Lake Femund; the route drops into it off Grådalen and turns west at about km 40. Mauritz Landhandel (Kopparleden 6075, on the old copper-road route 26) is the valley's country store and the practical reason to swing by: staffed daytime, self-service access around the clock outside staffed hours, useful if you pass outside shop hours. Resupply context: after this the on-route options before Alvdal (km 163) are thin; DNT's self-service Ellefsplass cabin at km 75 stocks provisions for members, and the Bergset shops in Rendalen (Joker and Coop Marked) are a ~3 km detour off the track near km 125.
pois.detour: About 2 km south of the route's westward turn at km 40

pois.name: Femundsmarka National Park (across Lake Femund)
pois.description: Famous wilderness of glacier-scoured pine forest and lakes on the EAST side of Femunden, Norway's third-largest lake and largest natural one (203 km2, about 60 km long); park established 1971. Geometry check: the route never touches it. Closest practical access is Synnervika quay at the lake's north end (coordinates given), about 10 km east of the track near km 30, where the heritage boat M/S Fæmund II (on the lake since 1905, bikes allowed) starts its daily round trip at 09:00 in season, calling at Elgå on the park side. Verdict: worth knowing it is there, but it only makes sense with a full extra day; on this itinerary, admire Femund's hills from the Grådalen-Tufsingdalen descent and keep rolling.
pois.detour: 10-12 km each way to Synnervika quay from track km ~30, plus boat crossing; not practical without adding a day

pois.name: Storlegda & Sølendalen high country (Rendalen)
pois.description: The wilderness heart of the leg. From Øversjødalen (km ~74, where DNT's self-service Ellefsplass cabin sits 150 m off track at km 75; 12 beds, provisions, open 1 June-10 Sept) the route climbs through intact seter country in Sølendalen, sheep and cattle grazing among working summer farms, tops out at about 1,270 m near km 111, then drops roughly 1,000 vertical metres off Storlegda into the Rendalen valley, the leg's low point at about 265 m near km 124. Rendalen markets itself on exactly these mountain gravel roads. The route passes within ~3 km of Bergset, Rendalen's municipal centre with two grocery shops (worth the detour for resupply); Otnes and the Jacob Breda Bull museum lie further south and are skippable. From the valley floor the route stays east of the Glomma and climbs directly over to Jutulhogget on Jutulhoggveien, joining the Glomma valley (Rv3) only after the canyon at km ~141.
pois.detour: On route, km 74-130

pois.name: Nordistusetra i Sølendalen
pois.description: A genuinely working dairy seter in Nordre Sølendalen that hosts visitors: dairy cows and calves on site, a simple guest cabin, traditional fare (milk cakes, coffee, smoked whitefish), fishing and paddling on the Sølna. The family is on the seter from around midsummer to early August, which matches a ~11-12 July pass-through. It sits down in the valley about 5.4 km off the high route near track km 97. Call ahead (+47 995 60 171) to confirm they are receiving visitors before committing to the detour; this is a working farm, not a cafe with posted hours.
pois.detour: About 5-6 km each way off route at km ~97, on valley seter roads; call ahead

pois.name: Jutulhogget canyon
pois.description: Billed by researchers as northern Europe's largest canyon (local sources say one of its longest), and the headline stop of the leg: 2.4 km long with walls up to 240 m. Carved in days, not millennia, by a catastrophic outburst flood of up to 180,000 m3/s when the ice dam of glacial lake Nedre Glomsjø (surface four times today's Mjøsa, volume close to Sweden's Vänern) collapsed roughly 10,400 years ago; a nature reserve since 1959 and one of the First 100 IUGS geological heritage sites. Geometry verified against the GPX: the route runs directly past the rim viewpoint at track km 140.5, on the climb out of Rendalen before the descent to the Glomma valley and Alvdal, so this is a zero-detour stop. Budget 20-30 min at the rim; a path leads partway into the canyon if legs allow.
pois.detour: None: the track passes the rim viewpoint at km 140.5

pois.name: Tronfjell (Tron, 1,666 m) via Tronfjellveien
pois.description: Free-standing massif towering over Alvdal with a gravel road (built in one month in 1959 for the NRK transmitter) climbing to near the summit, widely billed as Norway's second-highest road and a known strength-test climb: about 11 km and 1,160 vertical metres from the Alvdal side at around 10% average. Electronic card toll above Tronsvangen setergrend applies to cars; cyclists ride free. The summit panorama is said to take in seven parishes; the Flattron plateau halfway up carries the story of Indian philosopher Sri Ananda Acharya (Baral), who lived 28 years here (1917-1945) dreaming of a Peace University, and lies buried at about 1,400 m. Honest verdict: from Alvdal (about 480 m) this is a half-day side trip on loaded legs; only for riders banking spare hours at the Alvdal stop or taking a rest day.
pois.detour: Side trip from Alvdal (track km 163): roughly 11-13 km and ~1,160 m climbing each way on the gravel toll road (free for bikes)

pois.name: Aukrustsenteret (Huset Aukrust), Alvdal
pois.description: Museum for Kjell Aukrust, Alvdal's own author-cartoonist behind Flåklypa Grand Prix (the most-watched Norwegian film ever), in a long, low landmark building by Pritzker laureate Sverre Fehn. Artist museum NOK 185 adult; museum shop and the Solan og Ludvig cafe are free to enter, and the Flåklypatoppen moon rocket (La Pollo XIII) runs daily in the school summer holidays from 22 June (NOK 75 supplement, 8 per tour). Open daily 10:00-16:00 in summer. Geometry verified: it sits about 100 m off the track at km 163.6, right where the leg ends, so even a cafe-and-shop stop is effectively free. Good end-of-leg morale stop before the climb out of Alvdal.
pois.detour: None: about 100 m off route at km 163.6 in Alvdal

poiGroups.verificationNotes:
Method: geometry checked computationally against the actual route track in /Users/hugh.brown/norway-bikepacking/route.json and rwgps_extract.json (22,110 points with elevation; haversine distance to every POI, plus course-point road names); facts checked against operator/official websites (rorosmuseet.no, kirken.no, femund.no, tronfjellveien.no, aukrust.no, opplevrendalen.no, ut.no) and independent sources (Wikipedia, sciencenorway, proff.no, local press). All 12 POIs exist; none fabricated; none removed.

PASSED CLEAN: Røros kirke (NOK 60, 1,600 seats, 1784, organ 18:00 from 23 June confirmed on kirken.no, and the suspicious-looking 8 June-11 July side-door renovation claim is real, confirmed on the parish notice board); Smelthytta (hours/prices confirmed but they are the published 2025 schedule, hedged accordingly); Slegghaugan; Olavsgruva (all prices, times, phone, 13 km, 50 m down/500 m in confirmed; note site shows 15:00 daily for 1-19 June 2026, not 'from 1 June' open-ended); Nordistusetra (every detail confirmed: dairy cows, milk cakes, smoked sik, Sølna paddling, midsummer-early Aug season, phone); Mauritz Landhandel (Kopparleden 6075, staffed daytime + 24h self-service confirmed in local press); Fæmund II (1905, 09:00 daily from Synnervika, bikes allowed all confirmed); Aukrustsenteret (NOK 185, NOK 75 rocket, 10-16 daily, Fehn, km 163.6 at ~110 m off track).

FIXED: (1) Grådalen POI was in the wrong valley: its coordinate (62.5031, 11.5579, track km 13.5) sits in Hådalen on Femundsveien; Grådalsveien only begins at km 18.3 and Grådalen runs from Rambergsjøen down to Tufsingdalen at ~km 40. Moved coordinate to on-track 62.387, 11.696 (km 30) and corrected km range from 8-22 to 18-40. Also removed the 'signed Seterveien network' claim: Seterveien runs Røros-Dalsbygda-Vingelen, west of Røros, a different corridor. (2) Femunden is Norway's THIRD-largest lake (largest natural), not second-largest. (3) Storlegda POI had three numeric errors: the leg tops out at 1,267 m at km 111 (not ~1,050 m at km 102); the descent is ~1,000 vertical metres (not 700); and Bergset is only ~3 km from the track at km 125 (not '8+ km south'), which also matters for resupply (Joker + Coop Marked there). (4) The 'route crosses the Glomma near Hanestad (km ~129)' claim is false: the track stays east of the Glomma, climbing from the Rendalen valley directly to the Jutulhogget rim on Jutulhoggveien and only joining Rv3 in the Glomma valley at km 141.1. Jutulhogget's framing corrected to match. (5) Jutulhogget: length 2.4 km and 240 m depth confirmed, but dropped the unverified 150-300 m width; replaced the '140 km3, about three Mjøsas' volume (140 km3 is ~2.5 Mjøsas, and unsourced) with sciencenorway's actual comparison (surface 4x Mjøsa, volume near Vänern); 'northern Europe's largest' kept but attributed, since en.roros.no itself only says 'one of the longest'. (6) Tronfjell: 'seven church steeples' corrected to 'seven parishes' (sju prestegjeld, per local sources); climb corrected from '13-15 km / ~1,200 m' to ~11 km / ~1,160 m at ~10% (climbfinder/pjamm); added that the toll is free for cyclists; road-built-1959-for-NRK and Baral dates (1917-1945 = 28 years) confirmed. (7) Tufsingdalen: removed the false 'nothing between here and Alvdal' claim (Ellefsplass DNT self-service cabin with provisions sits 150 m off track at km 75.2, Bergset shops ~3 km off at km 125) and the garbled 'valley name nods to Kopparleden' sentence (the valley is named for the Tufsinga river; Kopparleden is the road). Ellefsplass corrected from 'unstaffed' to self-service (12 beds, provisions, open 1 June-10 Sept). (8) Removed unverifiable puffery 'probably the only mine dump on the UNESCO list' from Slegghaugan. (9) Minor distance trues-up: Smelthytta is ~200 m off track (not under 100 m), Aukrustsenteret ~100 m (not 80 m), Jutulhogget rim at km 140.5.

CAVEATS: Smelthytta and Olavsgruva 2026 summer hours/prices were not yet published at check time (June 2026); figures shown are the latest published. Røros kirke summer hours quoted (Tue-Sat 11-16) are the 2025 pattern. Aukrust hours confirmed for June; July assumed same per 'school holidays daily' pattern.

pois.name: Grimsdalen protected landscape and Grimsdalsvegen toll road
pois.description: Roughly 40 km gravel mountain road through what Norwegians market as 'the world's most beautiful seter valley', a protected landscape (landskapsvernomrade) between Rondane and Dovrefjell. Used for livestock and seter traffic since the 1600s; the valley holds working summer farm clusters (Tollevshaugen is the largest with 11 seters; Verkensetrene with the old Pondervangen trading site), Viking-age graves (one near the Mesaetre road dated to ~900) and large reindeer mass-trapping systems. Automated toll for motor vehicles (plate camera via youpark.no), nothing to pay on a bike; officially open about 5 June to 1 December, with sections occasionally night-closed for wild reindeer. The track rides its full length, entering from the Folldal side.
pois.detour: On route, approx km 190-245

pois.name: Grimsdalshytta (DNT) with Rondane panorama
pois.description: Historic DNT Oslo og Omegn lodge mid-valley with what ut.no calls an incredibly beautiful view toward Rondane's majestic peaks across the valley. 54 beds plus a 19-bed self-service annex; staffed with food service from roughly mid-June to mid-September (self-service rest of year outside reindeer calving season). The natural overnight or lunch stop in Grimsdalen at ~km 230.
pois.detour: On route, km ~230

pois.name: Vegaskillet rest area (Grimsdalsvegen/Haverdalsvegen fork)
pois.description: Rest stop at the road fork on the western section of Grimsdalsvegen where the side road to Haverdalseter branches off (vegaskillet means 'the road divide', not a watershed). Benches, a couple of small lakes and fishing water make it a documented break spot, about 6 km up from the Eftansae toll point on the Dovre side; you pass it on the long descent toward Dovre/Gudbrandsdalen. Note: the viewpoint name 'Gravbekkleitet' from older route notes could not be verified; Vegaskillet is the documented stop.
pois.detour: On route

pois.name: Rondane National Park (northern edge)
pois.description: Norway's first national park (1962), home to wild reindeer and the iconic 2178 m Rondslottet. The route does not enter the park but rides its northern boundary through Grimsdalen, with constant views of the peaks; cycling inside the park is restricted to designated routes. Marked DNT trails into the park (e.g. toward Doralseter) start at Grimsdalshytta if you want a foot detour.
pois.detour: Views on route; entering the park requires a hike from Grimsdalshytta

pois.name: Musk ox safari, Dovrefjell (Hjerkinn/Dombas)
pois.description: Dovrefjell holds Norway's only wild musk ox herd, one of very few in Europe and by far the easiest to reach. Guided safaris meet at Hjerkinn at 09:45 for a 10:00 start and walk 7-15 km over roughly 3-7 hours (typically ~6) depending on where the herd is. Not on the route: Hjerkinn is ~30 km northeast of Dombas up the E6, but it is one train stop away (four daily trains each direction on the Dovre line), so the practical option is a half-rest-day from Dombas (km 264). Book ahead for a July Saturday.
pois.detour: Off route: ~30 km each way from Dombas, or one train stop to Hjerkinn; plan a half day

pois.name: Fokstumyra Nature Reserve
pois.description: Norway's first nature reserve (protected 1923) and a Ramsar wetland (2002) on the Dovrefjell plateau: ~7.5 km2 of marsh with ~170 recorded bird species including bluethroat, common crane, hen harrier, ruff and short-eared owl. A 7 km loop trail, partly boardwalk, is the only permitted way through the reserve, so it costs real time. Worthwhile mainly for birders; others get better value staying on the route.
pois.detour: Off route: ~11 km north of Dombas on the busy E6 each way, plus the 7 km walking loop

pois.name: Sladalsvegen high mountain road
pois.description: 32 km summer-only toll gravel road (toll for motor vehicles only; normally opens the first Monday after 17 May) from Lesja over the plateau to Vagamo, topping out at 1180 m on an old seter and livestock track. From the open plateau you see mountains in five national parks: Dovrefjell-Sunndalsfjella (Snohetta), Dovre, Rondane, Jotunheimen and Reinheimen, followed by a long descent into Vaga. This is the route's marquee gravel sector between Dombas and Vaga (roughly km 280-310). Essentially no services on top (at most a seter serving simple food in season); carry water.
pois.detour: On route, summit roughly km 295

pois.name: Vagakyrkja (Vaga Church)
pois.description: One of Norway's oldest wooden churches: originally a stave church from ~1150, rebuilt 1625-27 as a cruciform church reusing the medieval materials, including carved stave-church portals and decorative wall planks. Inside are an early-Gothic crucifix from the mid-1200s, a pulpit from ~1630 and a 1674 altarpiece. Right in Vagamo centre at km 311, a 15-minute stop alongside resupply.
pois.detour: On route in Vagamo centre

pois.name: Jutulporten rock gate
pois.description: A ~25 m high rock face just above Vagamo where the light-coloured stone forms what looks like an arched gateway into the mountain, home of the jutul (mountain troll) in the tale of Jutuln and Johannes Blessom recorded by Asbjornsen. Reached by turning off at the church and following the road ~800 m, then bearing right and left toward Ovre Nordherad for a few hundred metres more. Quick, free and pairs naturally with the church and museum.
pois.detour: ~1-2 km off route from Vagamo centre plus a short walk

pois.name: Jutulheimen bygdamuseum
pois.description: Vaga's small open-air folk museum near Jutulporten, with historic log buildings and exhibitions on reindeer-hunting legend Jo Gjende and woodcarver Jo Visdal. Important caveat: in summer the buildings and exhibitions are shown by appointment only (arrange via Gudbrandsdalsmusea), so it is not a drop-in stop; contact ahead if you want a tour.
pois.detour: On route in Vagamo, ~500 m from centre

pois.name: Klones historic farm estate
pois.description: Farm estate on Vagavatnet documented since the 1500s; the great Storofsen flood of July 1789 destroyed about half the farm's assets. English gentleman-explorer Eardley John Blackwell (who came around 1850 with Theodore Rathbone to explore Jotunheimen, befriending hunter Jo Gjende) bought Klones in 1859 and built the grand English manor-style main house in 1867-73. An agricultural school for ~90 years (1919-2009); today it offers rooms, apartments, breakfast and dining. A practical sleep option ~2 km south of Vagamo on Rv15, essentially on the route toward Randsverk.
pois.detour: On or immediately beside the route, ~2 km south of Vagamo

pois.name: Ridderspranget gorge (Sjoa river)
pois.description: Dramatic slot gorge where the glacier-green Sjoa river is squeezed between rock walls, named for the medieval legend of the knight Sigvat from Valdres leaping the chasm with the bride he had stolen from Ivar Gjesling of Sandbu. The side road leaves Rv51 5 km south of Randsverk, runs ~1 km to a parking area with picnic tables and toilets, then a 250 m walk to the edge. If the GPX turns off Rv51 into Murudalen at Randsverk, this is roughly a 12 km round-trip detour; verify the exact turn against the track. Do not attempt the jump.
pois.detour: ~12 km round trip from the Randsverk junction plus 250 m walk (verify against GPX)

pois.name: Jotunheimvegen / Vinstre plateau panorama
pois.description: The 45 km summer-only toll gravel road (toll for motor vehicles; open roughly late June until the snow comes in October, so confirm status if riding early season) between Skabu and Rv51 near Bygdin crosses an open mountain plateau and follows the north shores of Sandvatnet and Vinstri (Vinstre), with big-sky views to the Jotunheimen peaks, possible sightings of the local herding cooperative's reindeer, and small mountain farms along the lake. This is the scenic payoff of the Murudalen-Vinstre leg (roughly km 370-414). Note: the Besseggen ridge itself is not visible from here; its trailhead at Gjendesheim lies ~25-30 km off route via Rv51 from Randsverk and is a full-day hike, not realistic mid-tour.
pois.detour: On route along Vinstre's north shore (coordinates approximate)

poiGroups.verificationNotes: All 15 POIs exist and sit in the right valleys; none removed. Verified against ut.no, grimsdalsvegen.no (official road operator), aukrust.no/sverrefehn.info, klones.no, haugseter.no, Bane NOR, Wikipedia, jotunheimen.com, fjordblick.com, sykkelriket.no and bucketlistnorway.com. Corrections made: (1) Aukrustsenteret was renamed Huset Aukrust in 2019; Sverre Fehn attribution and Alvdal location confirmed. (2) Grimsdalsvegen official season is 5 June to 1 December, not early October; ~40 km, plate-camera toll via youpark.no, night closures for wild reindeer (NRK 2023) all confirmed; Tollevshaugen (11 seters, largest cluster), Verkensetrene, Viking grave ~900 AD and mass trapping pits confirmed via Dovre sources. (3) Grimsdalshytta: 54 beds plus a 19-bed self-service annex, staffed mid-June to mid-September, DNT Oslo og Omegn, Rondane view confirmed by ut.no. (4) Vegaskillet is a road fork (Haverdalsvegen junction), not a watershed; benches, lakes and fishing confirmed, ~6 km above the Eftansae toll point; unverified 'panoramic Rondane view' and 'swim break' claims removed; coordinates plausible for the junction but not pinpoint-verified. (5) Rondane facts (first NP 1962, Rondslottet 2178 m, cycling restricted to designated routes, DNT trail from Grimsdalshytta) all check out. (6) Musk ox: softened 'Europe's only easily accessible herd' to Norway's only wild herd; meet time is 09:45 for 10:00 start, ~6 h/7-15 km typical, confirmed by operators; Hjerkinn has four daily train stops each way (Bane NOR), one stop from Dombas; unverified 'Dombas pickup' claim removed. (7) Fokstumyra: 1923 first reserve, Ramsar 2002, ~170 species, 7 km boardwalk loop as only access all confirmed; area corrected to ~7.5 km2. (8) Sladalsvegen: 32 km, 1180 m summit, toll for motor vehicles (~100 NOK), opens first Monday after 17 May; sykkelriket lists five visible national parks (adds Dovre NP), description updated. (9) Vagakyrkja: every date confirmed by Wikipedia (1150 stave origin, 1625-27 rebuild, mid-1200s crucifix, ~1630 pulpit, 1674 altarpiece); coordinates exact. (10) Jutulporten: ~25 m height and Blessom legend confirmed; access directions refined per fjordblick. (11) Jutulheimen: significant fix, summer visits are by appointment only via Gudbrandsdalsmusea, not drop-in; Jo Gjende exhibition confirmed. (12) Klones: Storofsen 1789 (half the farm's assets lost), Blackwell purchase 1859, manor 1867-73, school 1919-2009, current rooms/dining all confirmed; wording tightened (Blackwell bought it, Rathbone was his fellow explorer).
