(13) Ridderspranget: legend corrected to Sigvat from Valdres stealing Ivar Gjesling's bride (the 'of Kvie' epithet appears only in some tellings); 5 km south of Randsverk turn-off, parking with picnic tables and toilets, 250 m walk all confirmed. (14) Jotunheimvegen: 45 km Skabu-Bygdin, Sandvatnet/Vinstri shores confirmed; Wikipedia gives opening as late June (not relevant for mid-July but flagged); 'grazing reindeer herds' softened to the local herding cooperative's animals; Besseggen caveat correct. (15) Haugseter: 140-year lodge, ~74 beds across apartments and cabins, restaurant, boat rental, open 19 June-September confirmed via haugseter.no; unverifiable 'Store Nuten trapping pits' claim removed. Route km markers (163, 230, 264, 295, 311, 385) are from the caller's track and were not independently verifiable; detour distances were checked for plausibility against map geometry and all pass.

logisticsNorth.gettingThere:
RØROS START (main variant), Thu 9 July 2026. No direct Oslo-Røros train; you connect at Hamar. Earliest workable chain, re-verified on Entur for that exact date: Vy regional RE10 Oslo S dep 06:28 -> Hamar arr 07:44, change to SJ Nord R60 (Rørosbanen) Hamar dep 08:11 -> Røros arr 11:38. Total 5h10, riding by midday. This is the only morning option: the next R60 north from Hamar is 12:11 (Røros ~15:38, verified). The official R60 timetable (valid 14 Dec 2025-12 Dec 2026) confirms the 08:10/08:11 Hamar departure runs Mon-Fri only; on Saturdays the first train is 10:11, so do not slip the start to a weekend. One nuance the official timetable adds: all R60 trains are marked 'R - mandatory seat reservation', so buy your (dynamically priced) train tickets ahead even though the bike itself cannot be reserved. Bikes: on Vy RE10 you roll the bike into the carriage marked with a bike symbol and buy a bike ticket on board (Vy: 50% of adult single, min NOK 27, max NOK 275). On SJ Nord R60 bike space is not reservable: per SJ it is conductor's discretion, space permitting, hung on hooks in the goods compartment (max 175 cm length, 5 cm tyre width); SJ bike ticket is 50% of a Standard Flexible adult fare, min NOK 50 / max NOK 250. Practical mitigation for two riders: board early and travel midweek; Thursday morning is low-risk. Buy at sj.no, vy.no or Entur as one through ticket.

DOMBÅS START (short variant), same day. SJ Nord F6 (Dovrebanen, Oslo-Trondheim) train 43: the printed timetable says Oslo S 08:02 -> Dombås 11:58 daily; Entur shows 07:50 -> 11:58 on 9 July 2026 (summer-works adjustment, so re-check Entur near the date). ~4h, riding by noon, i.e. both variants start at the same time. CRITICAL difference, confirmed in the official timetable legend: F6 has mandatory seat reservation ('R') AND mandatory bike-space reservation ('On departures Oslo-Trondheim you need to reserve a space for your bicycle. You can purchase a ticket online at Entur'). SJ does not publish a capacity figure; the widely cited 5 bike spaces per set (10 when two sets are coupled) is from norwaybybike.com, unofficial. Book both bikes the moment plans firm up (sj.no or Entur, 'bicycle' add-on). Fallback: night train 405, printed Oslo S 22:50 Wed 8 July -> Dombås 03:36; Entur shows 22:49 -> 03:29 for that date. Also bike-reservable.

One warning that applies to BOTH operators: if planned engineering work puts a section on rail-replacement bus, neither SJ nor Vy carries bikes on the buses. Check sj.no/sanntid/planlagt-arbeid and Entur the week before.

Recommendation: the 06:28 Oslo start works for both variants (the Dombås rider takes the 07:50/08:02 F6 from Oslo S directly). If you cannot secure F6 bike spaces for Dombås, the Røros start is reservation-free by design.
logisticsNorth.bailouts:
The route touches rail at nine points on three lines, which is what makes the dynamic 12-day/9-day call possible. The one commit zone: between Dombås (km 264) and Gol (~km 510) there is no railway for roughly 250 km over the Vågå-Vinstre-Beitostølen-Slettefjell-Vaset section. Decide your pace plan at Dombås at the latest; after that the next bailout is Gol. All times below re-verified against Entur for the actual July 2026 dates.

1. RØROS, km 0 - Rørosbanen (R60, SJ Nord). Weekdays 4 trains south towards Hamar/Oslo (04:18, 08:22, 12:21, 16:23) and 3 north to Trondheim (05:00, 15:44, 19:45). Thinner Sat/Sun. No bike reservation possible; conductor decides if there is space (regional diesel units, a handful of bike hooks). Seat reservation is mandatory, so buy the passenger tickets as soon as you decide. Bike ticket 50% of flexible fare, NOK 50-250.
2. ALVDAL, km 163 - Rørosbanen (R60). Weekdays 4 southbound, Entur-verified: 05:15, 09:19, 13:20, 17:20. Saturday: 07:15, 13:20, 17:20. Sunday: 13:20, 15:16, 17:20. Change at Hamar to Vy RE10 for Oslo. Same no-bike-reservation regime.
3. DOMBÅS, km 264 - Dovrebanen (F6, SJ Nord). NOT uniform across the week, and your Dombås decision day is the weekend of 11-12 July: Mon-Fri towards Oslo 08:47, 10:54, 12:43, 15:52, 18:12 + night 02:31 (Entur shows 02:18 in July); SATURDAY only 10:59 and 18:12; SUNDAY 10:54, 12:43, 15:52, 18:12 and no Saturday-night train, so nothing before 10:54 on Sun 12 July. Towards Trondheim Mon-Fri ~12:01, 14:05, 18:14, 20:10, 21:59 + night. Mandatory bike reservation (5 spaces per set per norwaybybike, unofficial) - the only bailout where you must pre-book, so book via the SJ app/Entur as soon as you decide. Loophole if F6 bike spaces are gone: Vy RE10 starts from Dombås at 05:23 towards Oslo/Drammen (runs Mon-Sat, not Sunday, Entur-verified) - Vy regional rules, no bike reservation, buy the bike ticket on board.
4. GOL, ~km 510 - Bergensbanen (F4, Vy). Better served than first thought: 6 day trains each way + night. July-verified eastbound to Oslo 09:58, 11:54, 15:45, 17:45, 19:29, 20:55 (+ night 03:25); westbound 09:20, 11:16, 12:18, 15:04, 17:21, 19:27. Vy bike rules apply here and at all Bergensbanen points: mandatory bike reservation, 15 spaces per train, summer flat fare NOK 275 regardless of distance, bike must be labelled with destination/name/phone (Bergen Line only). Hard caveat: bikes are NOT carried on Bergensbanen rail-replacement buses - check Entur before relying on it.
5. GEILO, ~km 560 - Bergensbanen (F4). Best-served bailout, all six eastbound verified for July: 09:16, 11:12, 15:04, 16:51, 18:48, 20:13 to Oslo; westbound to Bergen 09:59, 11:56, 13:00, 13:31 (R40), 15:49, 18:01, 20:11.
6. HAUGASTØL, ~km 587 (Rallarvegen start) - Bergensbanen; only a subset of F4 stops plus the summer R40. Direction matters here: WESTBOUND (Bergen) 4 daytime trains - 10:25, 13:50 (R40), 16:13, 18:25; EASTBOUND (Oslo) only 14:39 and 19:48 daytime + night 02:18, plus a 13:05 R40 shuttle to Geilo. Treat as a half-frequency Geilo and plan direction accordingly.
7. FINSE, ~km 614 - Bergensbanen, no road access (rail or Rallarvegen only). 6 eastbound to Oslo (July-verified: 08:38, 10:31, 14:20, 15:51, 18:06, 19:27) and ~8 westbound incl. R40s, connecting at Myrdal to Flåmsbana (verified: 11:20 -> Myrdal 11:47 -> R45 12:06 -> Flåm 13:04). If weather closes the Finse-Myrdal high section, the train IS the bailout - but bikes need the Vy reservation, and spaces from Finse in July are contested by Rallarvegen traffic; book the night before.
8. MYRDAL - junction Bergensbanen/Flåmsbana, no road access. Flåmsbana (R45) 9 departures/day each way in July, Myrdal deps 09:30-19:35, roughly every 75 min.
9. FLÅM, km 705 - Flåmsbana terminus. R45 Flåm deps 08:20-18:25, 9/day in July. Bike on Flåmsbana: NOK 280 (verified on vy.no), reservation mandatory when you buy the ticket, only bookable while Rallarvegen is open in summer (15 spaces). For the homeward leg, Flåm -> Myrdal -> Oslo or Bergen, book bikes on both the R45 and the F4.

Net: Rørosbanen and the Oslo-Hamar leg never need bike reservations (show up, low risk midweek); Dovrebanen and everything from Gol westward always do. Two refinements to the decision logic: Dombås thins to 2 Oslo trains on Saturdays and nothing before 10:54 on Sundays, and the 05:23 RE10 from Dombås (Mon-Sat) is the reservation-free escape hatch if F6 bike spaces are full.
practicalNotes: Grimsdalsvegen (Folldal->Dombås section): private toll road run by Grimsdalsvegen SA, automated number-plate tolling via YouPark for motor vehicles - 2025-season prices were raised to car NOK 110 single passing, MC NOK 60, season pass NOK 1,000 (pay at youpark.no within 48h of passing). Tolling is plate-recognition of motor vehicles; bicycles pass free. Normally opens ~5 June and closes ~1 Dec, so it will be open 9-12 July; it runs through the Grimsdalen landscape conservation area with DNT's staffed Grimsdalshytta at ~km 230 (meals/beds, good Sunday fallback).

Slådalsvegen (Lesja->Vågå, km ~270-300): ca 32 km summer-open gravel toll road, highest point 1,180 m (Tatterhøi), municipal road owned by Lesja and Vågå. Toll is charged to motor vehicles at the Viflaten bomstasjon; cyclists ride free (exact car tariff not published online - irrelevant on a bike). Already open for the 2026 season: Vågå municipality announced it open on 28 May 2026. It was the 2013 E6 detour road, so the surface is car-passable gravel throughout.

Hike-a-bike, Røros->Alvdal: no documented mandatory carry on the northern leg, but the route creator (Martin Storli) is explicit that the route is 'long descents and correspondingly long uphills' on seter tracks and that without low gears there may be many long hike-a-bikes - budget pushing on the steepest Orvdalen/Storlegda ramps. The only two confirmed hike-a-bike spots on the whole route are further south, both storm-Hans damage (Aug 2023): Hinøglslivegen between Sikkilsdalsvegen and Jotunheimvegen (Murudalen/Vinstre section, short carry) and Høgspenten between Nysetvegen and Sangefjellvegen (after Slettefjell, several short carries). Both passable with a bike per the route page (updated Feb 2025; note the RideWithGPS page is JS-rendered and was not re-verifiable by crawler - re-read it in a browser before departure).

Mobile coverage: expect normal 4G in every valley town (Røros, Tufsingdalen, Alvdal, Dombås, Vågå, Beitostølen, Gol, Geilo) and long dead or marginal stretches on the high seter roads - especially Grådalen/Orvdalen/Storlegda (Røros->Alvdal, ~100 km of wilderness), Grimsdalen, and Murudalen/Vinstre. There is no authoritative dead-zone list; check the operator maps before departure (telenor.no/dekning, telia.no/nett/dekning, ice.no/dekning/kart) for your SIMs. Emergency calls (112/113) roam on any available network even with no home-operator coverage; carry offline maps and consider one Telenor and one Telia SIM between the two of you, since their rural footprints differ.

Musk ox (Dovrefjell area, i.e. Grimsdalen->Dombås->Slådalsvegen days): verified against the official 'moskusvettregler' from Dovrefjell-Sunndalsfjella national park - keep minimum 200 m distance; a bull weighs up to 450 kg and runs 60 km/h. The rules also say: keep an overview and count the herd so you are not surprised by outliers, stay visible (never approach stealthily), and 'there is no shame in turning back'. If one paws the ground, tosses its head or snorts you are too close: back away calmly the way you came, never between animals, never towards calves. Bears: brown bears occur sparsely in the Femundsmarka/Østerdalen border forests (Tufsingdalen-Rendalen leg). Miljødirektoratet's advice: make noise on overgrown trails, never approach a carcass, and if you meet a bear, talk calmly and withdraw slowly - attacks on people are extremely rare. No food-hanging culture in Norway; just keep a tidy camp.

Resupply logic, north to south. Røros (last full town before 163 km of wilderness): Extra Røros (Havsjøveien 3, Mon-Fri 07-23, verified), Coop Mega Røros, and REMA 1000 (Falunveien 9) - verified SUNDAY-OPEN 10:00-23:00, the safety valve if you arrive or restart on a Sunday. Only shop before Alvdal: Nærbutikken Tufsingdal (Kopparleden 6075, ~km 55-60) - tiny Merkur-programme village store, partly self-service; do not rely on it for a full load. Alvdal km 163: Joker (Folldalsvegen 6666) + SPAR (Steia 16). Dombås km 264: Extra Dombås (Sentralplassen 5a, Mon-Fri 07-23, Sat 08-21, Sun CLOSED per Coop). Vågå km 311: Extra Vågå (Vågåvegen 47, weekdays to 23:00) - THE last proper grocery before Beitostølen km 427; the 116 km Murudalen/Vinstre/Bygdin stretch has only mountain lodges (Haugseter, Bygdin) selling meals. Beitostølen: Extra, KIWI (08-23) and SPAR. Then Vaset (small Joker), Gol and Geilo are full towns. Shop hours change with summer season - re-check in the chains' apps en route.

Sunday 12 July: standard Norwegian Sunday closing applies - supermarkets shut; only sub-100 m2 'brustadbu' stores, petrol stations and designated tourist-place shops trade (which is why REMA Røros can open). On the 12-day plan you will be around Grimsdalen/Dombås that day with Extra Dombås closed, so do the big shop Saturday 11 July in Alvdal (Joker/SPAR, Saturday hours) and treat Grimsdalshytta (DNT, serves dinner/breakfast) as the Sunday food stop. Note the rail implication too: no train leaves Dombås before 10:54 that Sunday. Beitostølen, as a tourist resort, is the most likely Sunday-open grocery cluster later in the trip - verify hours in the Coop/KIWI apps en route.

Camping gas (screw-thread canisters): Røros has two stockists - Intersport Røros (Mon-Fri 09-19, Sat 09-18, Sun closed) and Sport 1 Røros (Kjerkgata 15) - buy here, since you cannot fly with gas. Dombås: Sport 1 Jo Holum (Nordre Stasjonsvegen 26). Vågå is thinner: JT Sykkel (Moavegen 5A - also your only bike-spares stop between Dombås and Beitostølen) and Fjellkompaniet Vågå outdoor store; call ahead rather than assume. Beitostølen: Sport 1 Beito in Stølstunet. Net: one canister bought in Røros plus a Dombås top-up covers the trip.

Booking discipline summary: for the Røros start, no bike booking exists - but R60 seats are mandatory-reservation, so buy passenger tickets ahead; F6 Dombås bike spaces for the short variant ASAP (limited spaces, reportedly 5/set); Bergensbanen (NOK 275 flat, 15 spaces, label the bike) and Flåmsbana (NOK 280, 15 spaces) bike reservations the evening before any southern bailout or the final Flåm exit; remember the 05:23 RE10 from Dombås (Mon-Sat) as the no-reservation fallback. Re-check Entur (entur.no) and sj.no/sanntid/planlagt-arbeid the week before departure - neither Vy nor SJ carries bikes on rail-replacement buses.
